channel.title: OnOptions
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from OnOptions, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: A Rejected Candidate Keeps Demanding to Know Why I Didn’t Hire Him
item.description: Do I need to respond to his increasingly aggressive messages?

item.title: The 70/30 rule that separates millionaires from everyone else
item.description: "Sexy is how you go broke," David Bach argues, saying millionaires know something others don't: Boring is the real sexy.

item.title: The IOC is barring this Ukrainian athlete over messaging on his helmet at the Milan Cortina Games
item.description: Ukrainian skeleton athlete Vladyslav Heraskevych, a likely medal contender at the Milan Cortina Games , was barred from racing Thursday after refusing...

item.title: Allyson Felix Set Olympic Records. Then She Remade the Shoe Industry
item.description: The most decorated track and field athlete in history co-founded Saysh and designed the first running shoes made specifically for women’s feet.

item.title: This Cool iPhone App Can Instantly Transcribe Conversations in 100 Languages
item.description: Whisper Transcription, the latest iPhone app from Good Snooze, transcribes audio from meetings and voice memos in a wide variety of languages.

item.title: Google Gemini boss describes working with founders Larry Page and Sergey Brin to win the AI future
item.description: Demis Hassabis told Fortune it's a return to the "shipping culture" from "the golden era of Google."

item.title: How AI Deniers Are Setting Themselves Up for Failure
item.description: Whatever your opinion of AI, don’t fall into the trap of wishing it away. Because it’s not going anywhere.

item.title: Major staffing cuts are coming to D.C.’s Kennedy Center ahead of its 2-year closure
item.description: As the Trump administration prepares to close the Kennedy Center for a two-year renovation , the head of Washington’s performing arts center has warne...

item.title: The Labor Market Keeps Flashing Mixed Signals
item.description: January was either a great or terrible start to the year for jobs, depending on the data.

item.title: ‘Fertility president’ Trump has demanded a baby boom, and Stanford researchers have a solution: letting more people work from home, study finds
item.description: “It seems like just such an obvious thing to do,” remote work expert Nick Bloom said. “As an economist, I don't think I've ever seen the more obvious,...

item.title: HPE turns finance into the front line of enterprise AI
item.description: HPE’s AI named Alfred is shrinking reporting cycles and rewriting the CFO playbook.

item.title: Discord is asking for your ID. The backlash is about more than privacy
item.description: Want to use Discord from next month? You’ll have to hand over a photo of your ID or a scan of your face to verify you’re of age. It’s part of a new pr...

item.title: Why 50% stay broke and how one hour a day can change everything
item.description: Next time you go to work, David Bach says, try paying yourself first.

item.title: 5 things to know about Lockdown Mode, iPhone’s security feature
item.description: A little known security feature on iPhones is in the spotlight after it stymied efforts by U.S. federal authorities to search devices seized from a re...

item.title: Sweethearts has the most valuable real estate of all Valentine’s Day candy
item.description: Valentine’s Day may seem romantic, but to candy companies, it’s serious business. Our annual ode to St. Valentine is one of the most important and com...
